mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals immediate economic weakening and reduced corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline pattern yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over 5-year out-of-sample tests, or shows no predictive power compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strong economic momentum and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances during tightening cycles, or the signal reverses direction when rates exceed 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Surging monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor market fragmentation and weakening productivity, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over 5-year out-of-sample tests, or employment growth alone explains all predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.05) across multiple business cycles, or the signal decays within 2 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production signal immediate supply-side shocks and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 40% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when coincident with positive GDP revisions.
